name: The Cat Cafe
address: 302, Island Avenue, Asian Pacific Thematic Historic District, Gaslamp Quarter, Banker's Hill, San Diego, San Diego County, California, 92101, United States
coordinatePolicy: WGS84 coordinates from Nominatim manual seed; map rendered by map-creator/OSMnx.

poi.address: The Cat Cafe, 302, Island Avenue, Asian Pacific Thematic Historic District, Gaslamp Quarter, Banker's Hill, San Diego, San Diego County, California, 92101, United States

review: A downtown cat cafe stop with a classic check-in feel: easy to find, close to the Gaslamp Quarter, and good for a first field-guide entry.
reservationNote: Check current visit rules before going; timed visits may apply.
priceNote: Entry and drink policies may change, so verify on the cafe site before visiting.
environmentNote: Urban storefront energy with a simple cafe rhythm and adoptable-cat atmosphere.
photoNote: Good for storefront, table, and resident-cat snapshots if the room is calm.
visitTips: Best as the downtown starting point before moving north on the map.

cats.name: Miso
cats.review: Usually claims the calmest corner and makes the visit feel properly supervised.

cats.name: Button
cats.review: Small, fast, and very sure that every ribbon toy has been placed there personally.

name: Whiskers & Wine Bar
address: 2856, Adams Avenue, North Park, San Diego, San Diego County, California, 92116, United States
coordinatePolicy: WGS84 coordinates from Nominatim manual seed; map rendered by map-creator/OSMnx.

poi.address: Whiskers and Wine Bar, 2856, Adams Avenue, North Park, San Diego, San Diego County, California, 92116, United States

review: A North Park cat lounge entry with a more social evening mood, useful for a field guide that tracks both cats and hangout atmosphere.
reservationNote: Reserve ahead for busy evenings and weekend windows.
priceNote: Expect lounge-style pricing; confirm current packages before visiting.
environmentNote: Warmer and more social than a quiet cafe stop, with a neighborhood-night-out feeling.
photoNote: Good for cozy table photos, lounge details, and cats moving through the room.
visitTips: Works well as the most social stop in the guide.

cats.name: Olive
cats.review: The unofficial host, usually checking whether each guest has understood the room rules.

cats.name: Juniper
cats.review: A slower companion who makes the space feel less like a bar and more like a living room.

name: The Cat Lounge
address: 1006, Torrey Pines Road, Village of La Jolla, La Jolla, San Diego, San Diego County, California, 92037, United States

poi.resolved_name: The Cat Lounge Rescue and Adoption Center
poi.address: The Cat Lounge Rescue and Adoption Center, 1006, Torrey Pines Road, Village of La Jolla, La Jolla, San Diego, San Diego County, California, 92037, United States

review: A La Jolla rescue and adoption lounge that belongs in the guide as a cat-first destination rather than only a cafe stop.
reservationNote: Review adoption-lounge visit rules before arrival.
priceNote: Check current donation, entry, or booking expectations before visiting.
environmentNote: Rescue-lounge focused, calmer and more cat-centered than a typical coffee stop.
photoNote: Best for gentle portraits and quiet resident-cat moments.
visitTips: Treat this as the north-coast chapter of the map route.

cats.name: Maple
cats.review: A calm lounge presence who rewards patience more than speed.

cats.name: Pepper
cats.review: Prefers a good vantage point and may join only after deciding the room is acceptable.
